HELLO AND WELCOME It's been my privilege to serve as Member of Parliament for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages since 2017. ​ I'm proud to have served as the only Labour MP in Kent between 2017 and 2024 and the County's first ever female Labour MP. ​ I have lived and worked in Canterbury for more than 25 years and brought up my family here. I know first hand how Canterbury and Whitstable is an amazing area to live and work, and I appreciate the daily challenges faced by people across the Constituency. Hopefully you will find all the information you need here, but as you know politics is a fast moving business so for the latest news, sign up to my newsletter to stay in touch. 108K Constituency Population 60k+ Constituency cases since 2017 1 Kent's only independent voice in Parliament 8 Years working hard for you Latest News Latest SEND Survey Responses Rosie Duffield - Easter Newsletter Politics Inside Out: Rosie Duffield MP Rosie hosts a Meningitis Drop-In in Parliament About Rosie Find out more about Rosie and the values that guides her work as your Member of Parliament. Learn More Get in Touch Rosie can assist you with a range of issues as your local representative in Parliament. Contact Rosie Rosie's Priorities Find out more about Rosie's work across Canterbury, her campaigns in Parliament and how Rosie can help you with local issues and concerns. Read Now All the latest updates Keep up to date with Rosie's work in Canterbury and in Parliament. Read Now Working hard for you Rosie holds regular surgeries to support Constituents. Find out more STAY IN TOUCH! Sign up for Rosie's Newsletter SUBSCRIBE Thanks for submitting! pages.title: ABOUT ROSIE | Rosie Duffield MP
headings: h3: MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages
h2: HOLDING THE GOVERNMENT TO ACCOUNT .
pages.text_excerpt: --> ABOUT ROSIE | Rosie Duffield MP top of page Rosie Duffield MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages HOME ABOUT ROSIE PRIORITIES Defending our Green Spaces A Brighter Future for Our Young Building a Fairer Economy Protecting our Waterways Strengthening our NHS A Stronger Relationship with Europe CONSTITUENCY NEWS SEND Survey - April 2026 Birth Trauma Inquiry CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. ABOUT ROSIE Growing up in central London and moving to Kent in her 20s, Rosie Duffield has lived and worked in Canterbury and Whitstable for over 26 years. Rosie has worked as a Teaching Assistant in local schools, and with local charities, churches, parents’ groups, arts events and schools in the area. ​ As a working single parent, Rosie knows the impact of drastic cuts on this area from personal experience. Rosie really understands what life is like for those who need Tax Credits, benefits and decent housing. Rosie's politics is driven by her desire to make life better for the whole community, including those who fall on hard times, the homeless, refugees, people with disabilities, pensioners and families struggling to cope with the soaring cost of living. ​ Prior to being elected to Parliament in 2017, Rosie was Chair of the Canterbury branch of the Labour Party and its representative on local cross-party forums such as Canterbury For Europe, Amnesty’s Canterbury & District Human Rights Network, Kent Anti-Racist Network and the Canterbury Clean Air Group. YOUR MP SINCE 2017 . Rosie made history in 2017 when she beat the longstanding Conservative candidate to become the first female Labour MP in Kent - and only Labour MP in Kent from 2017-2024. Rosie has made cross party working a priority, conscious of being elected by a broad coalition of voters. ​​ Opposed to Brexit, Rosie was a leading figure in cross-party efforts to secure a People's Vote, and helped to organise a Parliamentary 'rebel alliance' that very nearly brought down the Government. Rosie runs a fully-staffed, busy office in the constituency to deal with local people's everyday issues. These include problems relating to housing, education, benefits, and immigration. Since being elected, her team of dedicated, caring caseworkers has dealt with tens of thousands of cases, providing a vital service to the people of the Canterbury area. As you may have seen, Rosie believes in speaking her mind. Some people don't like that but many more people do, reflected in Rosie's increased vote share in December 2019 . Nationally in 2019, Labour suffered its worst defeat in decades, whereas Rosie increased her majority ten times over, one of only 4 Labour MPs to do so.​​ HOLDING THE GOVERNMENT TO ACCOUNT . A current member of the Women and Equalities Select Committee , Rosie has previously served on the Work and Pensions and Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Committees. ​ ​ ​ Select Committees have a vital role in scrutinising the Government, holding Ministers, officials and big business leaders to account on your behalf. They commission investigations and publish reports on a wide range of issues that are relevant to everyday life. ​ Rosie has also served on Bill Committees and is particularly active in a wide range of All Party Parliamentary Groups (APPGs). Rosie chairs the APPG on Women's Rights, the APPG on Hazaras and is a longstanding member of the APPG against antisemitism. Rosie co-chairs the APPG on Birth Trauma, which published its groundbreaking Birth Trauma Inquiry Report in 2024 . ​ ​ ​ WORKING HARD FOR YOU . In September 2024, Rosie took the difficult decision to resign the Labour Party whip in Parliament. You can read Rosie's Resignation Letter here . She currently represents Canterbury constituency as an Independent Member of Parliament. ​ STAY IN TOUCH! Sign up for Rosie's Newsletter SUBSCRIBE Thanks for submitting! pages.title: SEND Survey - April 2026 | Rosie Duffield MP
headings: h3: MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages
pages.text_excerpt: --> SEND Survey - April 2026 | Rosie Duffield MP top of page Rosie Duffield MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages HOME ABOUT ROSIE PRIORITIES Defending our Green Spaces A Brighter Future for Our Young Building a Fairer Economy Protecting our Waterways Strengthening our NHS A Stronger Relationship with Europe CONSTITUENCY NEWS SEND Survey - April 2026 Birth Trauma Inquiry CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. SEND Survey - April 2026 The Government is planning on introducing new reforms it says will enhance support for children and young people with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND). You can find out more about the proposals here . I want to hear from you. Your experiences and insights into SEND are incredibly valuable and will play a crucial role in shaping my response. Please take a few minutes to complete this short survey by 12pm on Monday 13th April 2026. First name * Last name Email * Postcode * I am completing this survey because I am a... * Parent Teacher Medical Professional Service Provider Other Which education setting are you experienced in? * Mainstream Specialist Alternative Provision Early Years Post-16 Education Other 1. How would you describe your current experience of SEND? * 2. What do you think 'excellent' SEND provision looks like? * 3. To what extent do you agree that these proposed changes will positively impact children and their families? * Strongly Agree Agree Neutral Disagree Strongly Disagree 4. To what extent do you agree that mainstream schools will have enough funding and staff to provide extra support under the new system? * Strongly Agree Agree Neutral Disagree Strongly Disagree 5. The government has costed this reforms at £1.6bn for inclusion, £200m for training and £1.8bn to rebuilding local authority SEND teams. Do you think this amount is adequate to tackle the scale of reform required? * 6. Have you seen excellent SEND support in a Kent school that you think should be a model for others nationwide? * 7. What would you like decision makers to know about the needs of children requiring extra support? * 8. Is there anything else you'd like me to know about your experience of SEND? * If you do not wish to receive our newsletter, please tick this box. Submit Feedback STAY IN TOUCH! Sign up for Rosie's Newsletter SUBSCRIBE Thanks for submitting! Contacting Rosie As your local MP, Rosie can help and advise residents with various issues, such as education, environmental issues, antisocial behaviour, health and social services, highways, transport, housing, tax and national insurance, benefits, pensions and immigration. You can contact Rosie using the details below. ​ ​ By contrast, your Councillors can help constituents on various other issues, such as council tax, bin collections, housing issues, social services, schools, roads, and payments.​ ​ You can find your Canterbury City Councillor here , and your Kent County Councillor here . pages.title: Rosie hosts a Meningitis Drop-In in Parliament
pages.h1: Rosie hosts a Meningitis Drop-In in Parliament
headings: h3: MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages
pages.text_excerpt: --> Rosie hosts a Meningitis Drop-In in Parliament top of page Rosie Duffield MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages HOME ABOUT ROSIE PRIORITIES Defending our Green Spaces A Brighter Future for Our Young Building a Fairer Economy Protecting our Waterways Strengthening our NHS A Stronger Relationship with Europe CONSTITUENCY NEWS SEND Survey - April 2026 Birth Trauma Inquiry CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Latest News In Parliament In Canterbury Press Newsletter Search Rosie hosts a Meningitis Drop-In in Parliament Mar 24 1 min read Rosie (centre) pictured with the teams from Meningitis Research Foundation (left) and Meningitis Now UK (right) On Tuesday 24th March, Rosie Duffield MP hosted a drop-in for MPs and Peers in Parliament with Meningitis Research Foundation and Meningitis Now UK. The outbreak in Canterbury has shown the urgent need for Meningitis B (MenB) vaccination for sixth formers and students and the importance of raising awareness of the symptoms of meningitis. This drop in provided an opportunity for Parliamentarians to learn more about the disease and ask any questions they had. There is still no routine NHS MenB vaccination programme for teenagers and young adults. Rosie is calling on the Government to change this, along with a cross-party group of colleagues, many of whom attended the drop in. I was very grateful that they came in at very short notice to meet with MPs and Peers, answer their questions and inform us on what we can do to reduce spread of the disease and save lives. Meningitis Research Foundation and Meningitis Now do vital work across the UK and beyond, to raise awareness of Meningitis and conduct research. Tags: Canterbury Strengthening our NHS Meningitis Universities In Parliament Recent Posts See All Rosie leads a cross-party letter to the Health Secretary calling for a wider Men-B vaccination rollout Canterbury Meningitis Outbreak: House of Commons Statement Rosie calls on the Government to implement the Animals (Low-Welfare Activities Abroad) Act STAY IN TOUCH!
